title: The Master's Shortcut
description: Two wrong publications sit on the board. A master mix can expose one of them — but you still need to find it.

hints.text: You know ingredient 1's full alchemical from the clues. Ingredient 4 is only partially known. A master debunk works when one ingredient's true alchemical is definitively established — because then the audience can calculate exactly what the result should have been under the wrong publication.

Ingredient 1 is fully known (npN). Mix ingredients 1 + 4: the true result is neutral. If ingredient 4's publication (claiming NNN) were correct, the expected result would be mix(npN, NNN) = R−. That contradicts neutral — so ingredient 4's publication is exposed. Ingredient 1's publication can then be removed with a simple apprentice reveal.

Step 1: Master debunk — mix ingredients 1 + 4. Ingredient 1 is definitively known, so the audience can check ingredient 4's publication: mix(npN, NNN) = R− ≠ neutral → ingredient 4's publication removed. Step 2: Apprentice on ingredient 1 — reveal R (or G) → ingredient 1's publication removed.
